baseline.label: Baseline — current posture
baseline.description: Steady-state induction at MCLB Albany / Barstow / Blount Island. Backlog as inherited, parts on-hand at policy levels, single-shift normal posture.
baseline.top_bottleneck: Hydraulic seal kit NSN 4730-01-441-2298 — 0 on hand, 28d ETA from DLA Land
baseline.brief:
**BLUF** The single biggest bottleneck is **hydraulic seal kit NSN 4730-01-441-2298**, constraining four ground families across **ALB/BAR** and suppressing bay turns despite nominal labor capacity. If operators re-sequence inductions around seal-kit demand, protect the two kits on hand for FD-1/FD-2 completions, and defer transfer-case-dependent starts for 21 days, projected depot throughput improves by **+6 end items over the next 30 days** versus baseline.

## NAMED BOTTLENECK
**Hydraulic seal kit NSN 4730-01-441-2298 — ALB/BAR cross-family constraint.**  
Demand spans **MTVR, AAV, LAV, and M1A1**; only **2 kits on hand** against a backlog touching **68 ground end items** (MTVR 24, AAV 18, LAV 16, M1A1 10). This part blocks hydraulic closeout on multiple lines simultaneously and is the highest-leverage limiter on bay release; absent re-sequencing, expect **4-6 bay-equivalents** to idle intermittently while work-in-process accumulates.

## PARTS AVAILABILITY — CASCADING EFFECTS
- **NSN 4730-01-441-2298, Hydraulic seal kit** — immediate long pole for **MTVR/AAV/LAV/M1A1**; with only **2 on hand**, likely caps near-term completion to **~2 priority ground units** unless kits are tightly allocated.
- **NSN 2520-01-562-9981, Transfer case** — **0 on hand, 21d ETA**; directly suppresses **MTVR/LAV** completions at **ALB/BAR**, making early induction of transfer-case candidates poor use of bay space.
- **NSN 1730-01-498-7102, Hydraulic actuator, ramp assy** — **4 on hand, 60d ETA**; constrains **AAV** and some **M1A1** closeouts, so BAR/ALB should avoid over-inducting ramp/actuator-heavy candidates.
- **NSN 1680-01-651-2244, Prop rotor blade** — **0 on hand, 21d ETA**; hard stop for **MV-22** at **BIC**, especially on aircraft already in late-stage structural/mechanical flow.
- **NSN 3110-01-617-8013 / 2935-01-510-9923, MV-22 prop hub bearing / fuel control** — **0 on hand, 14d / 7d ETA**; jointly create staggered holds at **BIC**, reducing practical MV-22 completion rate unless non-parts-limited inductions are pulled forward.

## TOP 5 ACTIONS (NEXT 30 DAYS)
1. **ALB:** Reserve both on-hand **4730-01-441-2298** kits for the two highest-priority near-complete **MTVR/M1A1** jobs, yielding **+2 completions / 30 days**.  
2. **BAR:** Freeze induction of any **MTVR/LAV** requiring **2520-01-562-9981** until D+21 and backfill with non-transfer-case AAV work, avoiding bay lock and adding **+1 unit / 30 days**.  
3. **BIC:** Pull forward **MV-22** inductions that do **not** require **1680-01-651-2244** or **3110-01-617-8013** in first-pass scope, improving aircraft flow by **+1 unit / 30 days**.  
4. **BAR:** Allocate the **4 on-hand 1730-01-498-7102** actuators to **FD-1/FD-2 AAVs** only, preventing lower-priority consumption and generating **+1 completion / 30 days**.  
5. **ALB/BAR:** Stand up a daily cross-depot parts board for **4730 / 2520 / 1730** candidate matching and WIP triage, reducing avoidable idle bay time for **+1 unit / 30 days**.

## ALTERNATIVE INDUCTION SEQUENCES
- **Option 1:** Pull **2 MV-22** inductions forward at **BIC**; defer **2 LAV** inductions at **BAR** pending **2520** arrival — **net +2 units / 30 days**, trade-off is deeper LAV queue at BAR.  
- **Option 2:** Prioritize **FD-1/FD-2 AAV** at **BAR/BIC** using available **1730** stock; defer **2 M1A1** at **ALB** awaiting hydraulic kits/sights — **net +1 to +2 units / 30 days**, trade-off is lower armor-line utilization at ALB.  
- **Option 3:** Keep **ALB** focused on non-transfer-case **MTVR/M1A1** tear-down and subassembly while **BAR** absorbs additional AAV induction — **net +1 unit / 30 days**, trade-off is higher WIP age on selected MTVR/LAV hulls.

## CLASSIFICATION
UNCLASSIFIED // FOR OFFICIAL USE.

surge.label: Surge — Force Design 2030 push
surge.description: MARFORPAC Stand-In Forces backlog draw-down: workforce surged 1.4x via overtime + reservist augmentation; held-parts pool released; priority weights tilt toward FD-1/FD-2.
surge.top_bottleneck: Bay 4 hydraulic lift availability — MCLB Albany
surge.brief:
**BLUF:** **Bay 4 hydraulic lift availability — ALB** is the single biggest throughput limiter: one constrained lift is suppressing cross-family heavy repair flow for MTVR/LAV/M1A1 despite the 1.4x workforce surge and released held-parts pool. If operators re-sequence inductions around Bay 4, shift common hydraulic work to BAR, and protect scarce long-pole parts for FD-1/FD-2 jobs, projected uplift is **+8 to +10 completed end items over the next 30 days**.

## NAMED BOTTLENECK
**Bay 4 hydraulic lift availability — MCLB Albany (ALB).**  
ALB carries **14 bays / 2 shifts** and is the primary heavy-flow node for **MTVR, LAV, and M1A1**; Bay 4 is the pacing resource for lift-dependent hydraulic/powertrain work across those families. With surge manning, labor is no longer the first constraint; Bay 4 is effectively capping ALB at roughly **1 lift-dependent induction every 3–4 days**, leaving an estimated **3–4 additional ALB jobs** unrealized in 30 days unless work is re-sequenced.

## PARTS AVAILABILITY — CASCADING EFFECTS
- **NSN 4730-01-441-2298 — Hydraulic seal kit:** **2 on hand, 7-day ETA**; common to **MTVR/AAV/LAV/M1A1** and now the main cross-platform pacing part for lift work, risking delay to **6–8 inductions** if consumed outside FD-1/FD-2 priorities.
- **NSN 2520-01-562-9981 — Transfer case:** **0 on hand, 21-day ETA**; constrains **MTVR/LAV** powertrain closeout at ALB/BAR, likely holding **3–5 vehicles** in WIP and tying up bays.
- **NSN 1680-01-651-2244 — MV-22 prop rotor blade:** **0 on hand, 21-day ETA**; hard-stops BIC **MV-22** completions, reducing likely 30-day output by **2–3 aircraft** unless non-blade work is front-loaded.
- **NSN 1730-01-498-7102 — Hydraulic actuator, ramp assy:** **4 on hand, 60-day ETA**; limits **AAV/M1A1** ramp-related jobs, especially BAR AAV and ALB M1A1, with probable suppression of **4 completions** if not rationed.
- **NSN 3110-01-617-8013 — MV-22 prop hub bearing:** **0 on hand, 14-day ETA**; creates a second serial dependency at BIC, stacking with rotor blade shortages and slowing **MV-22 induction throughput**.

## TOP 5 ACTIONS (NEXT 30 DAYS)
1. **ALB:** Reserve **Bay 4** exclusively for FD-1/FD-2 **MTVR/LAV/M1A1** lift-dependent tasks for 30 days, yielding **+3 completions**.  
2. **BAR:** Shift **2–3 MTVR/LAV hydraulic jobs** from ALB to BAR using released held-parts stock, freeing ALB lift time for **+2 completions**.  
3. **ALB/BAR:** Allocate **NSN 4730-01-441-2298** only to jobs within **72 hours of closeout**, preventing WIP sprawl and adding **+1 to +2 completions**.  
4. **BIC:** Front-load **MV-22** teardown, fuel-control, and non-rotor work ahead of **NSN 1680-01-651-2244 / 3110-01-617-8013** arrival, enabling **+2 aircraft** to complete rapidly once parts land.  
5. **BAR/ALB:** Fence **NSN 1730-01-498-7102** to highest-priority **AAV/M1A1** jobs only, avoiding low-priority consumption and preserving **+1 to +2 completions**.

## ALTERNATIVE INDUCTION SEQUENCES
- **Option 1:** Pull **FD-1/FD-2 MTVR and LAV** forward at **ALB/BAR**; defer **2 FD-3 M1A1** inductions at ALB — **net +4 units / 30 days**; trade-off is temporary M1A1 backlog growth.
- **Option 2:** Pull **MV-22** non-rotor inductions forward at **BIC**; defer **2 AAV** inductions at BIC until ramp-actuator allocation is clear — **net +3 units / 30 days**; trade-off is slower AAV queue burn-down.
- **Option 3:** Move **2 ALB LAV** inductions to **BAR** and hold **1 low-priority MTVR** pending transfer-case arrival — **net +2 to +3 units / 30 days**; trade-off is slightly higher BAR bay utilization.

## CLASSIFICATION
UNCLASSIFIED // FOR OFFICIAL USE.

parts_constrained.label: Parts-constrained — long-pole NSN slip
parts_constrained.description: Three long-pole NSNs (hydraulic seals, MV-22 prop blade, transfer case) slip 30 days. Workforce normal. Tests parts cascading effects.
parts_constrained.top_bottleneck: Hydraulic seal kit NSN 4730-01-441-2298 — 0 on hand, 28d ETA from DLA Land
parts_constrained.brief:
**BLUF:** **Hydraulic seal kit NSN 4730-01-441-2298 is the single biggest bottleneck across ALB/BAR/BIC, constraining four ground-vehicle families simultaneously and driving the largest queue growth over the next 30 days.** If operators re-sequence inductions around seal-kit demand, protect the two available kits for FD-1/FD-2 completions, and shift near-ready work toward MV-22 tasks not gated by blades/bearings, projected depot completions can improve by **~6 end items in 30 days** versus current induction order.

## NAMED BOTTLENECK
**Hydraulic seal kit NSN 4730-01-441-2298 — ALB/BAR/BIC network constraint.** Deterministic optimizer identifies **0 effective on-hand / 28-day ETA from DLA Land** as the governing constraint; scenario data shows only **2 kits immediately usable** against demand spanning **MTVR, AAV, LAV, and M1A1**. This single NSN can stall work across **68 of 80** backlog items by family applicability, making it the dominant throughput limiter versus bay or labor capacity.

## PARTS AVAILABILITY — CASCADING EFFECTS
- **NSN 4730-01-441-2298, hydraulic seal kit:** gates **MTVR/AAV/LAV/M1A1** teardown-to-reassembly flow; likely suppresses **~4-6 ground-vehicle completions** in 30 days if consumed on low-priority starts.
- **NSN 2520-01-562-9981, transfer case:** **0 on hand, 21-day ETA**; directly slows **MTVR/LAV** powertrain closeout at **ALB/BAR**, likely holding **~3-4 units** in WIP late in month.
- **NSN 1680-01-651-2244, MV-22 prop rotor blade:** **0 on hand, 21-day ETA**; hard-stops final assembly/test on **MV-22** at **BIC**, likely deferring **~2-3 aircraft-equivalent completions**.
- **NSN 3110-01-617-8013, bearing, prop hub MV-22:** **0 on hand, 14-day ETA**; compounds blade shortage and reduces ability to finish otherwise labor-ready **MV-22** inductions.
- **NSN 1730-01-498-7102, hydraulic actuator, ramp assy:** **4 on hand, 60-day ETA**; constrains **AAV/M1A1** tail-end completion, especially if seals are allocated first and ramp actuators become the next hold point.

## TOP 5 ACTIONS (NEXT 30 DAYS)
1. **ALB/BAR/BIC:** reserve the **2 immediately available 4730-01-441-2298 kits** for the highest-readiness **FD-1/FD-2** units already >70% complete, yielding **+2 completions**.
2. **BAR:** defer induction of **2 new AAVs** requiring **1730-01-498-7102** and pull forward non-ramp AAV work packages, avoiding dead-bay occupancy and adding **+1 completion-equivalent**.
3. **ALB:** postpone **MTVR/LAV** starts forecast to need **2520-01-562-9981** before day 21 and substitute **M1A1 optical-sight-ready** jobs, improving flow by **+1 unit**.
4. **BIC:** sequence **MV-22** inductions to complete inspections, fuel-control work, and non-rotor tasks before blade demand, preserving line utilization for **+1-2 units WIP advancement**.
5. **BAR/ALB:** cap new seal-kit-dependent ground inductions at **available-kit + 1** until confirmed receipt, reducing WIP congestion and recovering **~+1 completion** through better bay turnover.

## ALTERNATIVE INDUCTION SEQUENCES
- **Option A:** Pull **MV-22 non-blade work** forward at **BIC**; defer **2 LAV** inductions at **BAR** pending transfer cases — **net +2 units / 30 days**, trade-off: more aircraft WIP awaiting final parts.
- **Option B:** Prioritize **M1A1** at **ALB** using available optical sight and one seal kit; defer **1 AAV** and **1 MTVR** — **net +1-2 units / 30 days**, trade-off: BAR ground backlog ages.
- **Option C:** Freeze all new seal-kit-dependent starts depot-wide for 14 days; fill bays with teardown/inspection-only jobs — **net +1 unit / 30 days**, trade-off: lower apparent induction count but better completion yield.

## CLASSIFICATION
UNCLASSIFIED // FOR OFFICIAL USE.
